Title at top left: “Anatomy of Motor Pathways: Spinal Cord”

Central diagram of patellar reflex / stretch reflex:
- Left: lower leg bent at knee, thigh vertical, lower leg hanging down, foot pointing slightly forward
- Label near upper thigh: “Quadriceps femoris muscle”
- Small reflex hammer striking patellar tendon below knee
- Label by hammer: “Hammer stretches quadriceps”
- Dotted outline of prior/lower leg position behind current leg
- Curved dashed arrow showing forward extension of lower leg after reflex

Within upper thigh muscle:
- Blue elongated structure labeled “Muscle spindle (stretch receptor)”

Neural pathway:
- Blue afferent fiber from muscle spindle to spinal cord cross-section on right
- Label along blue fiber near cord: “Sensory neuron (dorsal root)”
- Red efferent fiber from ventral horn region of spinal cord back to quadriceps
- Label near red fiber: “Alpha motor neuron (ventral root)”

Right side:
- Beige spinal cord cross-section with butterfly-shaped gray matter in center
- Blue sensory fiber enters dorsally and connects in spinal cord to red motor neuron
- Red motor neuron exits ventrally back to muscle

Top right overlay controls from video player:
- blue circular button with white curved arrow
- icons/text: “Hide”, “Exit full screen”, “Options”, “Help”, “Close”

Title at top: **Anatomy of Motor Pathways: Spinal Cord**

Central diagram of a knee-jerk reflex arc:

- Left: bent human leg, thigh and lower leg visible, foot pointed slightly downward
- Small reflex hammer near patellar tendon with label: **Hammer stretches quadriceps**
- Dotted outline behind lower leg indicating prior position; curved black arrow under foot indicating forward kicking movement

Upper thigh muscle labeled: **Quadriceps femoris muscle**
- Arrow points to anterior thigh muscle

Near upper thigh, small receptor structure inside muscle labeled: **Muscle spindle (stretch receptor)**

Right: spinal cord cross-section
- Outer pale region and inner butterfly-shaped gray matter
- Blue sensory pathway from muscle spindle to spinal cord labeled: **Sensory neuron (dorsal root)**
- Path enters dorsal side of spinal cord

Inside ventral horn, red motor neuron cell body and red axon projecting out to muscle
- Label near exiting red pathway: **Alpha motor neuron (ventral root)**

Flow depicted:
- Hammer stretches quadriceps
- Muscle spindle activated
- Sensory neuron carries signal through dorsal root into spinal cord
- Synapse onto alpha motor neuron in ventral horn
- Alpha motor neuron exits via ventral root back to quadriceps
- Resulting quadriceps contraction extends lower leg

Title at top: “Complex movement with just a spinal cord”

Top subfigure:
- Left-to-right side view schematic of central nervous system.
- Labels:

- Pink spinal cord extending to a pink brain at right; thin black nerve lines descending from spinal cord.

Bottom subfigure:
- Left side: two stacked activity traces in boxed panels.
  - Upper panel label: “Extensors”
  - Lower panel label: “Flexors”
- Extensors trace: red horizontal baseline with bursts/spikes.
- Flexors trace: blue horizontal baseline with bursts/spikes alternating with extensor bursts.
- Below traces: gait phase bar labeled “Stance” under left portion and “Swing” under right portion, separated by alternating red and blue triangular segments.
- Black lines from traces/projected nerves connect to hind leg muscles of an animal.
- Right side: partial side view of a cat’s hindquarters and hind legs standing/walking on a treadmill belt.
- Label near lower back: “Spinal cord resection”
- Treadmill platform shown in gray with front roller/wheel visible at bottom.

Overall academic content:
- Spinal cord disconnected from brain by resection.
- Hind-leg movement on treadmill associated with alternating extensor and flexor activity across stance and swing phases.
